channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Mon, 20 Jul 2026 16:14:36 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Biohacker Bryan Johnson claims ice packs boost male fertility: Does science agree?
item.description: Bryan Johnson, a longevity influencer, claims that icing his testicles greatly increases his sperm count. His unique approach has inspired several Redditors to experiment with this method for improving fertility. Although some research hints at the benefits of mild scrotal cooling for male fertility, experts caution that there is insufficient evidence to suggest significant improvements in testosterone or overall sperm production.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Scientists find toxins from Florida's toxic algae blooms travel through air, raise risk of ALS
item.description: Recent research reveals the presence of cyanobacterial toxins in the air of Southwest Florida, matching those found in dolphins displaying Alzheimer's characteristics. This airborne contamination indicates that inhabitants may be exposed to lower concentrations of these toxins, which are believed to heighten the chances of ALS and various ailments. Unlike food-related toxins, mitigating exposure through breath poses a significant and complex challenge.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: HasanAbi says he may file a restraining order against Destiny after latest livestream comments
item.description: In a troubling turn of events, HasanAbi is weighing the option of filing a restraining order against fellow creator Destiny due to what he describes as continuous harassment stemming from Destiny's supporters. He has accused this community of orchestrated harassment and doxxing attempts. HasanAbi insists that this issue is not just about him but is essential for the protection of others.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Indian-origin scientist Satchin Panda resigns from California's Salk institute amid sexual misconduct probe
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A Quebecer noticed a strange circular pit on Google Maps; scientists confirm it's a crater
item.description: An amateur astronomer found a circular depression on a map. Scientists investigated and confirmed it as a meteorite impact crater. This ancient crater is approximately 390 million years old. The discovery adds Uhackatik to Canada's list of impact structures. Modern tools enable everyday people to contribute to scientific understanding.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Scientists find sudden ‘switch-off’ in Sun’s cycle
item.description: Scientists have developed a new method to predict solar cycle strength. This approach identifies a precise point where extreme space weather abruptly ceases. Researchers can now forecast the next solar cycle's activity up to seven years in advance. Early predictions suggest Solar Cycle 26 may be moderate in strength. Further refinement of these forecasts will occur in approximately two years.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Why activists threw balloons full of acidic mixture on Microsoft's upcoming data centre in Amsterdam
item.description: AI data center construction is igniting a storm of environmental and social dilemmas. Community activists are protesting against these facilities, highlighting their substantial energy and water usage. Tensions have risen in Amsterdam, with protests turning into acts of vandalism. Residents living nearby complain of noise issues and land seizures. Tech firms find themselves facing mounting backlash from activists and impacted local property owners.
item.dc:creator: TOI Tech Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Nico Williams: "I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother. Because she once escaped death and…" - an emotional tribute to his mother, the extraordinary journey behind his success and the sacrifices that shaped a football star
item.description: Spanish footballer Nico Williams honored his mother after Euro 2024 victory. He gave his medal to her, recognizing her difficult journey. His mother escaped death and crossed the Sahara Desert from Ghana to Spain. This arduous migration paved the way for his and his brother's careers. Williams highlighted his mother's sacrifice as more deserving than his own athletic achievement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Final: IShowSpeed hypes BTS halftime show; backstage photo lights up X
item.description: Popular streamer IShowSpeed enjoyed one of the biggest moments of the FIFA World Cup final after watching BTS perform during the halftime show. The viral streamer later met the K-pop group backstage. The internet personality became a trending topic as videos of his hilarious reaction spread across social media during the championship match between Spain and Argentina.
item.dc:creator: Global Sports Desk

item.title: TheMightyBA's Streamer University 2026 controversy explained as Ludwig speaks about his future
item.description: The controversy at Streamer University 2026 surrounding TheMightyBA intensified when footage surfaced of him patting MeesterKeem's head, triggering a storm of criticism. Ludwig stood by TheMightyBA, suggesting that redemption is possible, drawing parallels to IShowSpeed’s remarkable evolution. With persistent effort and time, creators can address their past errors and forge a path to redemption.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: In 1928, a German architect planned to block the Strait of Gibraltar, drain the Mediterranean and create a new continent called Atlantropa
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists reveal whether 400,000-year-old rock fragments be the oldest known evidence of human fire-making
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Meet Wally Funk: She passed NASA's astronaut tests but waited 60 years to reach space
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Japan's forest bathing idea in 1982: 40 years of research found a more complicated picture
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: In 2020, a couple bought an abandoned 1830s house at auction and found a snake-filled property; 5 years later, they revealed how they turned it into a family home
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: More than 2,200 years ago, an ancient Greek scholar calculated the Earth's circumference with a stick and the Sun
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Spain and Portugal have been hiding a second rabbit species for nearly 2 million years
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Debris of asteroid shattered 800 million yrs ago may have bombarded Earth for 150 million yrs
item.description: A new study suggests that a giant asteroid breakup in the main asteroid belt about 800 million years ago may have triggered a long period of impacts across Earth, the Moon and Mars. Researchers linked the event to the Eulalia asteroid family and say it could explain ancient lunar craters. While the impacts coincided with major climate and biological changes on Earth, the study does not prove they caused them.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: How Amazon soy pact collapse could affect Brazil
item.description: A voluntary soy deforestation pact has collapsed after nearly two decades of success. This collapse is predicted to cause significant future deforestation in the Amazon rainforest. Researchers estimate over one million hectares of forest loss by 2036. This deforestation will release substantial carbon emissions equivalent to Canada's annual output. Aggressive enforcement and stronger import demands are now recommended to protect the Amazon.
item.dc:creator: TOI Lifestyle Desk

item.title: Two North Dakota men discover they were switched at birth, raised by each other's families for nearly four decades before a Christmas gift changed everything
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
